value.reason: The cited page is explicitly and entirely about the "Hooks" feature for OpenAI Codex — an extensibility framework that injects custom scripts into the agentic loop at named lifecycle events. This matches the capability under audit exactly and is distinct from adjacent capabilities (project-scoped instruction files, packaged plugins, output/prompt customization, model selection). The label "Hooks" is the canonical name used throughout the page. The description accurately reflects the page: lifecycle event names (PreToolUse, PostToolUse, SessionStart, Stop, etc.) are all confirmed; configuration via hooks.json or config.toml at user or project level is confirmed; the matcher + command handler structure is confirmed; concurrent execution of matching hooks is confirmed; and the trust/review requirement via /hooks for non-managed hooks is confirmed. No invented claims are present.
